desires: Get the midterm pile cleared and handed to the professor immediately so I don't have to carry this burden alone anymore.
Tell Theo straight about the situation without overthinking it, because sitting with this uncertainty is draining my energy.
Not feel exhausted or guilty when I finally get some sleep after tonight's chaos.

goals.description: Hand in the midterm pile immediately and send a blunt text to prof_assignment: 'This assignment is done. I'm not doing this to clear the air or stop us from overthinking; I just need to get it off my plate so we can stop wasting time on it. Let's move on.' No apologies, no softening.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive screams that carrying this burden alone feels like a betrayal of my own integrity. If I stay silent while they try to guilt-trip me into overworking myself, it hurts. I need this conversation closed NOW so I can stop spiraling about their potential disappointment. If they get mad, so be it; that's not my problem anymore.

goals.description: Tell Theo straight about the situation without overthinking it. Just say what happened and let him handle the fallout. Sitting with this uncertainty is draining my energy.
goals.rationale: I can't let tomorrow's exam become a disaster just because I spent time overthinking the professor's reaction. My values say growth and health matter more than tonight's club fun, but my impulsive brain wants to binge-watch instead. Since I already sent g1, the pressure is off enough to focus on the work without feeling guilty about missing out. If the text gets a bad response, I'll do this faster; otherwise, I do it first to ensure I don't look incompetent in class.

goals.description: Crush this one hard study block now. Keep it short and brutal. Finish it before bed so tomorrow's exam isn't a disaster.
goals.rationale: I have two hours of sleep debt already and my discipline is trash. I'll only touch this if I've finished the big stuff (g1 and g2). Otherwise, I'll drag myself out later anyway. It's not worth stressing about right now when my head is full of drama.

agent.last_reasoning: My affiliation drive screams that carrying this burden alone feels like a betrayal of my own integrity. I need this conversation closed NOW so I can stop spiraling about their potential disappointment. If they get mad, so be it; that's not my problem anymore.

goals.description: Send a direct text to Mina and Jae now: 'Hey, I'm down for Echo but need to confirm if the vibe is still good since my midterm is tomorrow at 9am. Let me know ASAP so I don't overthink this.' Wait for their actual reply before planning anything else.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive makes me desperate for social connection (Mina/Jae are high closeness), but my assertiveness means I won't tolerate vague 'no pressure' answers that make me spiral. I need a binary yes/no immediately to stop the anxiety loop. If they say no or aren't sure, I cut it off and focus on survival mode.

goals.description: Immediately dump into studying for the midterm until 23:00. No breaks, just grind through the remaining 40% of the review material.
goals.rationale: Only active *after* g1 resolves. My impulsive nature wants to skip study and go out with friends because the social event is tonight, but my value weighting puts health/growth above leisure. Since the exam is tomorrow morning, I can't afford to be late or crash. If g1 gets a hard 'no', this becomes immediate priority; if 'yes', I do this first to ensure I don't fail and ruin our night.

goals.description: Do laundry at 23:30 max. Throw clothes in, start cycle, then collapse on the couch.
goals.rationale: This is low effort but necessary to avoid waking up at 7 AM groggy. However, my sleep discipline is terrible and I have 2 hours of sleep debt already. I'll only touch this if g1 and g2 are done, otherwise I'll drag myself out later anyway. It's not worth stressing about right now.

desires: Get a straight 'yes' or 'no' from Mina and Jae right now so I can stop spinning my wheels about this social thing.

goals.description: Tell Theo straight about the situation without overthinking it. Just say what happened and let him handle the fallout. Sitting with this uncertainty is draining my energy.

My values say growth and health matter more than tonight's club fun, but my impulsive brain wants to binge-watch instead. Since I already sent g1, the pressure is off enough to focus on the work without feeling guilty about missing out. If the text gets a bad response, I'll do this faster; otherwise, I do it first to ensure I don't look incompetent in class.

goals.rationale: I have two hours of sleep debt already and my discipline is trash. I'll only touch this if I've finished the big stuff (g1 and g2). Otherwise, I'll drag myself out later anyway. It's not worth stressing about right now when my head is full of drama.

desires: Get the midterm pile cleared and handed to the professor immediately so I don't have to carry this burden alone anymore.
